name: Apple Inc.
shortDescription: iPhone maker. The world's most valuable consumer hardware company, increasingly a services + AI play too.

name: Microsoft Corporation
shortDescription: Cloud (Azure), enterprise software, AI partner of OpenAI. One of the biggest beneficiaries of the AI build-out.

name: Alphabet Inc. (Class A)
shortDescription: Google Search, YouTube, Android, Google Cloud, Gemini AI, Waymo (self-driving). Cash machine + AI giant.

name: Amazon.com Inc.
shortDescription: Largest online retailer + AWS (the biggest cloud provider). Two very different businesses in one.

name: Meta Platforms Inc.
shortDescription: Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads. Heavy spender on AI and VR. Ad-driven revenue.

name: Netflix Inc.
shortDescription: Streaming pioneer. Now with ads, gaming, and live sports as growth bets.

name: Oracle Corporation
shortDescription: Enterprise databases pivoting to cloud (OCI). Major AI-infrastructure partner for OpenAI and others.

name: NVIDIA Corporation
shortDescription: The chip maker powering the AI boom. Their GPUs are the picks-and-shovels of the AI build-out.

name: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
shortDescription: CPU and GPU maker — the main competitor to Intel and Nvidia. Trying to catch up in AI chips.

name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
shortDescription: Defence and enterprise AI/data analytics. Used by US military, intelligence agencies, and large corporations.

name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
shortDescription: The world's biggest chip foundry. Makes the most advanced chips for Apple, Nvidia, AMD and others.

name: ASML Holding N.V.
shortDescription: Dutch monopoly on EUV lithography machines — the equipment needed to make the most advanced chips.

name: Broadcom Inc.
shortDescription: Designs custom chips for hyperscalers (Google, Meta) and networking gear. Major AI infrastructure player.

name: Qualcomm Incorporated
shortDescription: Designs Snapdragon mobile chips and modems. Most smartphones outside Apple use their tech.

name: Micron Technology Inc.
shortDescription: Memory chips (DRAM, NAND, HBM). Cyclical but the AI HBM boom has been transformative.

name: Intel Corporation
shortDescription: The legacy x86 leader. Trying a multi-year turnaround focused on foundry services and PC chips.

name: Salesforce Inc.
shortDescription: The biggest CRM software company. Pushing hard into AI agents for sales and customer support.

name: Adobe Inc.
shortDescription: Creative software (Photoshop, Premiere). Trying to defend its turf against AI image/video generators.

name: ServiceNow Inc.
shortDescription: Enterprise workflow automation platform. Strong AI agent narrative for IT and HR.

name: Snowflake Inc.
shortDescription: Cloud data warehouse. The 'data layer' a lot of enterprise AI workloads run on.

name: Datadog Inc.
shortDescription: Observability platform — monitors cloud apps. Benefits when cloud spending grows.

name: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Cybersecurity platform (endpoint protection). Premium valuation but consistent growth.

name: MongoDB Inc.
shortDescription: Document database platform — popular alternative to traditional SQL databases.

name: Tesla Inc.
shortDescription: EV maker turned AI / robotaxi / robot / energy company depending on how you squint. Wildly volatile.

name: Ford Motor Company
shortDescription: Classic Detroit automaker. Truck-heavy, struggling with EV transition costs.

name: General Motors Company
shortDescription: Largest US automaker by sales. Slow EV pivot but profitable ICE business.

name: Stellantis N.V.
shortDescription: Owns Jeep, Chrysler, Peugeot, Fiat, Maserati. European-American combo, currently under pressure.

name: Rivian Automotive Inc.
shortDescription: US electric truck/SUV startup. Loss-making but with Amazon as a big customer.

name: Toyota Motor Corporation
shortDescription: World's biggest car company by volume. Cautious on full EVs, leans hybrid — has been a winning strategy lately.

name: BYD Company Limited
shortDescription: Chinese EV + battery giant. Outsells Tesla in many markets. Hugely vertically integrated.

name: Mercedes-Benz Group AG
shortDescription: German luxury automaker. Premium pricing but slower in EV transition than Tesla/BYD.

name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
shortDescription: F-35 fighter jet maker, missiles, helicopters. The biggest US defence contractor.

name: RTX Corporation
shortDescription: Missiles, radars, jet engines (Pratt & Whitney). Diversified defence + commercial aerospace.

name: Northrop Grumman Corporation
shortDescription: Stealth bombers (B-21), nuclear missiles, satellites. Heavy on classified contracts.

name: General Dynamics Corporation
shortDescription: Tanks (Abrams), submarines, business jets (Gulfstream). Mix of defence + private aviation.

name: The Boeing Company
shortDescription: Commercial planes + defence. Multi-year struggle with 737 MAX safety issues and labour disputes.

name: Rheinmetall AG
shortDescription: German defence champion. Tanks, artillery, ammunition. Massive beneficiary of European rearmament.

name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
shortDescription: Largest US oil major. Big buybacks, leaning into LNG and chemicals.

name: Chevron Corporation
shortDescription: Second-largest US oil major. Dividend-focused.

name: Shell plc
shortDescription: UK-headquartered oil major. Cash-generative but climate transition is a slow-burn issue.

name: BP p.l.c.
shortDescription: British oil major. Recently scaled back its renewable energy push to focus back on hydrocarbons.

name: TotalEnergies SE
shortDescription: French oil major with a more substantial renewable energy bet than peers.

name: Enphase Energy Inc.
shortDescription: Solar microinverters + home batteries. Hit hard by high interest rates; recovery still uncertain.

name: First Solar Inc.
shortDescription: US-based utility-scale solar panel maker. Benefits from Inflation Reduction Act subsidies.

name: NextEra Energy Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US renewable energy producer + Florida utility. Steady dividend grower.

name: Plug Power Inc.
shortDescription: Hydrogen fuel cells. Highly speculative — has consistently lost money but still a hydrogen pure-play.

name: Booking Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Owns Booking.com, Priceline, Kayak. Cash-generative online travel agency.

name: Airbnb Inc.
shortDescription: Global short-term rentals marketplace. Strong brand, regulatory headwinds in major cities.

name: Marriott International Inc.
shortDescription: World's largest hotel chain (Sheraton, Westin, St. Regis, Ritz-Carlton…). Asset-light franchise model.

name: Delta Air Lines Inc.
shortDescription: Big US airline. Premium-focused strategy is paying off.

name: Intuitive Surgical Inc.
shortDescription: Makers of the da Vinci surgical robot system. Effectively a monopoly in robotic surgery.

name: ABB Ltd
shortDescription: Swiss industrial automation + robotics + electrification. Big in factory automation.

name: IonQ Inc.
shortDescription: Trapped-ion quantum computing pure-play. Highly speculative, not profitable. Big swings.

name: Rigetti Computing Inc.
shortDescription: Superconducting quantum computing. Tiny revenue, big narrative. Trade with caution.

name: D-Wave Quantum Inc.
shortDescription: Quantum annealing (different approach from gate-based). Niche use cases, very speculative.

name: Eli Lilly and Company
shortDescription: Maker of Mounjaro/Zepbound (GLP-1 weight loss drugs). Generational growth story unfolding.

name: Novo Nordisk A/S
shortDescription: Danish maker of Ozempic/Wegovy — the original GLP-1 weight-loss drugs. Worldwide demand.

name: Johnson & Johnson
shortDescription: Diversified pharma/medical devices giant. Slow steady grower with a strong dividend.

name: AstraZeneca PLC
shortDescription: UK/Swedish pharma giant. Strong oncology pipeline.

name: AbbVie Inc.
shortDescription: Maker of Humira (long the world's best-selling drug) plus Skyrizi and Botox. Strong cash flows.

name: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton
shortDescription: Louis Vuitton, Dior, Tiffany, Sephora, Moët, Hennessy… The luxury empire. Sensitive to Chinese spending.

name: Hermès International
shortDescription: Birkin and Kelly bags. The ultra-premium of the luxury world — outperforms because their pricing power is unique.

name: Compagnie Financière Richemont SA
shortDescription: Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, IWC, Piaget. Swiss watches + jewellery focused.

name: Ferrari N.V.
shortDescription: The supercar maker. Tiny production volumes + insane pricing power = a luxury business not really a carmaker.

name: MercadoLibre Inc.
shortDescription: The 'Amazon of Latin America' — e-commerce + payments (Mercado Pago). Strong growth in Brazil/Argentina/Mexico.

name: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd
shortDescription: Chinese e-commerce giant (Taobao, Tmall) + cloud + Ant Group. Hammered by regulatory crackdowns 2021-23.

name: JD.com Inc.
shortDescription: Chinese e-commerce with a focus on logistics and authentic-goods supply chain. Alibaba competitor.

name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
shortDescription: Largest US bank. Diversified — consumer banking, investment banking, asset management.

name: Bank of America Corporation
shortDescription: Big US consumer + commercial bank. Closely tied to US economic cycle.

name: The Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
shortDescription: Top investment bank — trading, M&A advisory, asset management. Cyclical earnings.

name: HSBC Holdings plc
shortDescription: Global bank with heavy Asia exposure (HK, China, Singapore). Pivoting back toward Asia.

name: DBS Group Holdings Ltd
shortDescription: Singapore's biggest bank. Consistent dividend payer, well-managed, regional Asia exposure.

name: Singapore Airlines Ltd
shortDescription: Premium long-haul carrier. Cyclical but Singapore's flag carrier with iconic service.

name: Singapore Telecommunications Ltd
shortDescription: Singapore telecom incumbent + stakes in regional telcos (Optus, Airtel). Dividend-focused.

name: Sea Limited
shortDescription: Singapore-headquartered: Shopee (e-commerce in SEA), Garena (gaming), SeaMoney (fintech). High growth, volatile.
